human_verification.method: manual spec-pack review plus local repository validation

source_classes_covered: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Current package task baseline from cloud-migration-specialist

promotion_criteria: sustained usage volume proves retained implementation materially improves cost, latency, or reliability
generated implementation passes the same scenario suite and functionality map with no unreviewed regressions
source refresh, tenant evidence retrieval, logging, rollback, tenant isolation, and boundary enforcement are tested in target runtime
human maintainer records separate promotion approval

notes: Manifest and evidence define a cloud migration specialist with explicit planning, scheduling, allocation, readiness, cutover, rollback, troubleshooting, and boundary behavior.

Scenario coverage includes strategy, dependency evidence, cutover runbook, readiness evidence, deployment and commercial packaging, troubleshooting, resilience, tradeoffs, capacity shortfall, priority conflict, final-approval refusal, out-of-scope rejections, low-confidence escalation, adjacent-lane routing, conflicting-source handling, and tenant adaptation.
The package is validated as a spec pack. It does not claim retained implementation ownership.
